_doc: Activity-driven adaptation rules. A lightweight analyzer reads output/metrics/hme-activity.jsonl, evaluates each rule against the recent N events, and writes exports into tmp/hme-adaptive-config.sh. Hooks source this file at fire time so behavior responds to observed patterns instead of fixed defaults.

_schema.window: how many recent events to consider
_schema.trigger: condition expression — see _eval() for allowed forms
_schema.export: shell variable to set when rule fires
_schema.value_when_fired: value exported when trigger is true
_schema.value_when_dormant: value exported when trigger is false (optional — omits export when missing)

rules.description: If the last 20 events show 3+ coherence_violation (edit_without_brief), auto-prompt i/hme-read on the next edit in the session.
When lots of productive events fire per turn, allow a slightly longer raw-tool streak before the HME-tool reminder (rewards focused work).
If the same 3 modules are briefed repeatedly, extend their KB cache TTL so we don't re-enrich on every read.
